QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_17

question.text: Which of the following is a ground for SEBI to refuse grant of registration to a merchant banker applicant?

answer.text: The applicant has more than 5 years of experience in financial markets
The applicant has 3 professionally qualified employees
The applicant has offices in multiple cities
A director of the applicant has been convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 6(g), if the applicant, or any of its directors, partners, or principal officers, has been convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude or economic offence, SEBI shall refuse registration. This is a mandatory disqualification ground. **The Trap:** Having more experience or more qualified employees than the minimum are positive factors, not grounds for refusal — candidates who misread the question might pick these. **Cross-Exam Context:** Moral turpitude and economic offence disqualifications apply across all SEBI intermediaries and are tested in NISM Series V-A, X-A, and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Due diligence on directors' backgrounds is essential before incorporating an entity for MB registration — one convicted director can sink the entire application.
